data.title: iScreen Introduces Bible-Themed Widgets for Easter: Daily Verses and an Immersive Reading Experience
data.description: Get ready to embrace the spirit of Easter with iScreen's latest Bible widgets, featuring a variety of daily verses, captivating video lock screens, and customizable home screen layouts. Recently launched in Los Angeles on March 27, 2026, iScreen, a renowned widget app for iPhone users worldwide, presents an array of innovative features to enhance your Easter celebrations. Whether you seek inspiration through daily scriptures or wish to personalize your device with unique lock screen designs, iScreen has you covered. Join the Easter festivities with iScreen's Bible widgets and elevate your iPhone experience.
Taiwan central bank governor stated that the bank may move toward policy tightening should inflation pressures increase.
WTO head noted that specific deadlines are in place to achieve a nimbler and more efficient trade body.
WTO head noted that the United States and Brazil are engaging with each other to try to reach a deal on the e-commerce moratorium.
The yuan began trading at 6.9166/USD versus Friday’s 6.9110 close.
Hong Kong’s benchmark index, the Hang Seng, is poised to drop 1.7% as markets open.
The head of Taiwan’s central bank said 2026 CPI could rise to 1.9% from the current 1.8% if average oil prices reach $100.
Hong Kong-listed Alibaba stock dropped 3.6% at the open.

From WSJ Trump is weighing a military operation to extract nearly 1,000 pounds of uranium from Iran, according to U.S. officials, a complex and risky mission that would likely put American forces inside the country for days or longer.
China cracks down on ‘bone ash’ burials in empty apartments-ft
WTO head noted that with the e-moratorium expired, countries can now apply tariffs on electronic products, though efforts are underway to reinstate it.
The yuan was pegged at its weakest midpoint since March 2 by PBOC.

data.title: Faraday Future Founder and Co-CEO YT Jia Shares Weekly Investor Update: The Company Wrapped up Its First Month of Robotics Deliveries as It Marches Forward Towards Its 200-unit Delivery Target for the First Delivery Quarter
data.description: In Los Angeles, Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. (NASDAQ: FFAI), a global Embodied AI (EAI) company based in California, provided a weekly business update by YT Jia, Founder and Global Co-CEO of FF. The update was filmed at their new Silicon Beach headquarters. Jia mentioned a personal milestone regarding admission offers from Harvard's CS program. Faraday Future's stock ticker is FFAI and Jia serves as the Global Co-CEO.
Malaysia’s main equity index slipped 1.2% to 1,692.4 points, closing at a level not seen since March 16.
2-year U.S. Treasury Bond yield is down 3.7 bps, trading at 3.879%.

Donald Trump said that his envoy to Belarus, John Coale, secured the release of 250 more political prisoners after talks with President Alexander Lukashenko, bringing the total freed since last May to over 500.
30-year JGB yields climbed 4.0 bps, now at 3.740%.
The People’s Bank of China fixed the yuan reference rate at 6.9223 against the dollar, up from Friday’s 6.9110.
1-month USD/INR forward contracts are quoted at 94.70, versus 95.35 on Friday.
